title: Tigers Crush Sri Lanka by 7 Wickets in Colombo Test Series Opener
content:
Bangladesh's pace attack delivered a commanding performance in Colombo on Sunday, dismissing Sri Lanka for just 134 runs in their first innings and then chasing down a modest 142-run target with seven wickets and more than a session to spare.

Taskin Ahmed was the standout performer, claiming five wickets for 38 runs to dismantle the Sri Lankan top order on a pitch that offered more assistance to pace bowlers than the hosts had anticipated. His opening spell of seven overs yielded three wickets without conceding a run, setting a tone of total dominance.

"I've been working hard on my length and the seam position," Taskin said in the post-match press conference. "The pitch was helpful but you still have to execute, and today the team executed as a unit."

Sri Lanka's batting lineup struggled to adapt to the conditions, with only Kusal Mendis offering meaningful resistance with a gritty 41. The hosts were bowled out in 34.2 overs, their lowest total against Bangladesh in a home Test match.

Bangladesh's reply was anchored by a composed half-century from Najmul Hossain Shanto, who brought up his fifty off 74 balls before falling to an edge behind the wicket. Mominul Haque and Mushfiqur Rahim then guided the Tigers to their target without further alarm.

The second Test in the three-match series is scheduled to begin in Galle on March 24. A Bangladesh victory in the series would mark their first-ever Test series win on Sri Lankan soil, a milestone that the dressing room is clearly targeting with focused resolve.
